content.claim: Whether Poland's reversal of 1999–2015 comprehensive education reforms contributed to the large PISA 2022 decline remains contested and unresolved; alternative explanations include persistence of reforms in teacher autonomy and family beliefs about tertiary attainment that were not reversed
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Causality is explicitly framed as an open question in the source, not established
Confounding factors (pandemic effects, test administration changes, international comparison shifts) not separated from reform effects
Multiple reform components were reversed at different times; aggregate effect cannot be attributed to single policy change
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
